message:
fix(raptorq): split truncation flags, overdetermined solver correctness, underdetermined fail-closed, poll_ready before send

Decoder: EliminationTrace truncation tracking split from a single
truncated flag into three independent flags: inactive_cols_truncated,
pivot_events_truncated, and strategy_transitions_truncated. The
block-Schur fallback retry path now only resets pivot_events_truncated
when clearing pivot history, preserving the inactive_cols_truncated
flag that was set before the retry. Test added verifying the flag
independence across fallback retries. RHS test data uses modular byte
values instead of bare cast to avoid u8 overflow on wide matrices.

Linalg: GaussianSolver::solve() and solve_markowitz() now return
solution vectors trimmed to the number of columns (variables) instead
of the full RHS vector. For overdetermined systems (rows > cols), the
extra RHS rows were included in the solution, causing downstream
consumers to misinterpret eliminated redundant rows as variable values.
New solved_result() helper handles the truncation. Underdetermined
systems (rows < cols) now return Singular instead of a partial solution
that leaves free variables undefined. Tests added for both cases.

Pipeline: poll_next_blocking now calls poll_ready on the sink before
poll_send, respecting the sink backpressure contract. Previously the
send was attempted without a readiness check, which could return
spurious WouldBlock errors on sinks that require explicit readiness
acknowledgement.

Proof: additional proof verification improvements.
Systematic: systematic encoder adjustments.
Tests: new test coverage for the above changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
